data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices architecture ensuring scalability and resilience.
data.technical_spec: Each service handles a specific functionality and communicates over REST or gRPC.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: YouTube utilizes global CDNs to deliver video content efficiently close to user locations.
data.technical_spec: CDNs such as Google's own network and other partners are used.

data.title: Database Sharding
data.description: Horizontal scaling across various database shards for handling large user data.
data.technical_spec: Relational databases are partitioned based on user data distribution.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Efficient traffic distribution across servers for optimal performance.
data.technical_spec: Software and hardware load balancers are used to manage incoming requests.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: YouTube operates over a cloud-based infrastructure to ensure high availability.
data.technical_spec: Google Cloud Platform powers various backend systems.
